rationale: The citing paper uses Boyd, Chandy, and Cunha (2010) in its core theory development, not just as background. It explicitly states that it is extending that work by studying CMOs in new ventures and VC funding, adding CMO education, and disaggregating role-specific experience. The paper also draws on Boyd et al.'s three CMO roles (informational, relational, decisional) to motivate its hypotheses and mechanisms. This indicates a direct build-on relationship.
relation_description: The citing study extends Boyd, Chandy, and Cunha (2010) from public-firm stock market reactions to CMO appointments to a new venture financing context. It preserves the CMO-performance logic while broadening constructs and contingencies (e.g., education, differentiated experience, environmental uncertainty) for VC funding outcomes.
